Working the cycle, four ways

You'll meet four pros, each already holding a recommendation. Read what they say back, work the cycle — acknowledge, identify, respond, read again — and decide when to close or convert. Some resolve fast. Others uncover something new underneath.

Activity complete

You worked the cycle, four different ways.

A recommendation was never the finish line — what you do with the reaction is the job. Every hesitation you just worked through was already answered somewhere in the conversation.

Ready to move on? Use the Continue button in the bottom-right corner to keep going.

Derek Washington — landscaper, Atlanta

Needs to process (Engagement Context). Recommended Premium, anchored to reaching 40 properties and finally affording to hire help (The "Because You Said" Structure).

"Whoa — that's a lot more than I was expecting to spend."

Step 1 — Acknowledge

Step 2 — Identify the driving dimension

Step 3 — Respond from the conversation

Step 4 — Read again

Derek: "Yeah… when you put it that way, I guess that tracks."

Premium — confirmed

Next step confirmed on the spot.

Rosa Kim — painter, Chicago

Skeptical (Engagement Context). Recommended Plus, anchored to needing two or three commercial accounts to fill December through February (The "Because You Said" Structure).

"I don't know… I've been burned by something like this before."

AE: "That sounds like it really stuck with you — I hear that."

Which Business Context dimension is actually driving this hesitation?

AE: "You mentioned the platform you tried before never converted — this time the ask is scoped to exactly what you need, and here's the specific data on how it performs for accounts like yours."

Leading with evidence and specifics is the move for a skeptical read — she's still in the conversation because she wants to be convinced.

Marcus Bell — HVAC technician, Dallas

Comparing options (Engagement Context). Recommended Premium, anchored to maintenance contracts on 200 homes for steady, year-round revenue (The "Because You Said" Structure). He mentioned earlier the decision is his alone.

"Let me talk this over with my wife before I commit to anything."

Step 1 — Acknowledge

Step 2 — Identify the driving dimension

Step 3 — Respond from the conversation

Step 4 — Read again

"Honestly… it's not really about her. I guess I'm just not sure about committing to this many months at once."

A new, more honest concern has surfaced. The cycle continues from here — same four steps, starting with acknowledgment again.

Jen Torres — general contractor, Denver

Flat energy (Engagement Context). Recommended Premium, anchored to aiming for roughly $2M in revenue in the next 18 months (The "Because You Said" Structure).

"I'm just really swamped right now — can we pick this up another time?"

Hesitation 1 — Timing

Step 1 — Acknowledge

Step 2 — Identify the driving dimension

Step 3 — Respond from the conversation

Step 4 — Read again

"…Okay, I guess. But I don't know if I'm ready to jump into something this big yet."

Hesitation 2 — Readiness (surfaces after timing is addressed)

Step 1 — Acknowledge

Step 2 — Identify the driving dimension

Step 3 — Respond from the conversation

Step 4 — Read again

"…Yeah. Okay. I think I'm ready — but I really do need to run for this next walkthrough."

The Conversion Signal

What do you do?

Thursday, 2pm — locked in

A committed return call is a close too — specific time, what needs to be different, and you own the next step.

Same cycle, four times — from the clearest case to the most complex. That's the job.
